items.title: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
items.summary: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

items.title: New era as Apple names new boss to replace Tim Cook after 15 years
items.summary: Ternus will take over running the technology giant in September as Cook steps up to become executive chairman.

items.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
items.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

items.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids
items.summary: Several people were also injured in the incident on Monday at the popular tourist site, say Mexican officials, weeks before the country hosts the Fifa World Cup.

items.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
items.summary: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

items.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.summary: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

items.title: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
items.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

items.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
items.summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket grounded after satellite 'mishap'
items.summary: The firm founded by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is investigating the failed launch.

items.title: Woman and child jumped off roof to escape deadly Louisiana shooting
items.summary: Authorities say a father fatally shot eight children, including seven of his own, in what appears to be a "domestic dispute".

items.title: More than 200 rescued from IS-linked group in DR Congo
items.summary: The youngest of those freed from an Allied Democratic Forces camp is a girl aged just 14, Uganda's army says.

items.title: Police gunfight with favela gang traps 200 tourists on hilltop
items.summary: The access route to Morro Dois Irmãos was blocked during the operation in Vidigal, leaving scores of alarmed sightseers stuck on the hill.

items.title: Trailblazing black beauty queen who defied apartheid South Africa dies aged 76
items.summary: At the 1972 Miss World contest, Cynthia Shange was one of two contestants from South Africa - one black and one white.

items.title: Days of Our Lives and Starship Troopers actor Patrick Muldoon dies aged 57
items.summary: The actor and producer also appeared in Melrose Place and Saved By the Bell.

items.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
items.summary: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.

items.title: What we know about the Iranian ship seized by the US
items.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian ship entering the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, US President Donald Trump has said.

items.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
items.summary: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

items.title: Nigerian wins global prize for trying to save bats in a country that shuns them
items.summary: Many Nigerians associate bats with witchcraft but that did not deter ecologist Iroro Tanshi.

items.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
items.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

items.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
items.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

items.title: Hot air balloon makes emergency landing in California backyard
items.summary: "Look at that, wow!" - A couple in Temecula woke up to find a balloon boarded with 13 people behind their home on Saturday.

items.title: Are insider traders making millions from the Iran war?
items.summary: The BBC has found significant spikes in activity shortly before the US president made some announcements.

items.title: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon
items.summary: The dramatic end to the Delaware Marathon occurred when the lead runner slowed in celebration before a trailing marathoner sprinted toward the finish line .

items.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
items.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.
